texto: Book-club night stopped abruptly when Caleb died. For almost a year and a half, as if by some type of tacit agreement, they all knew they couldn’t be in the same room at the same time. It was as if their collective grief would multiply, rebounding endlessly within any closed space like an image in a house of mirrors, until the pain would overcome them all.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Francis Guenette: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de passagem do tempo e prioridades. O trecho “Book-club night stopped abruptly when Caleb died. For almost a year and a half, as if by some typ…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que superacao, grief, love pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de boa-noite e luto.
